event: Saints Anthony the Hermit, Cornelius, and Mary Magdalene with a Donor
symbols: tau-shaped staff — Saint Anthony the Hermit's traditional attribute
bishop in red with mitre — Saint Cornelius, pope and martyr, in episcopal regalia
ointment jar held by Magdalene — her traditional attribute recalling the anointing of Jesus
kneeling donor — the patron who commissioned the painting, seeking the saints' intercession
golden halos — the sanctity of the three heavenly intercessors
description: Three standing haloed figures against a dark background with a small kneeling figure at their feet. At left, an elderly bearded man in a brown habit holds a tall tau-shaped staff — Saint Anthony. At center, a figure in elaborate red episcopal vestments and a pointed mitre holds a small object — Saint Cornelius. At right, a young woman in a green dress and white veil holds a small jar — Mary Magdalene. Between them at the bottom, a small kneeling figure in dark clothing (the donor) prays. The style is late Gothic with rich colors and careful detail.
theological_description: Lochner's painting presents three saints with a kneeling donor, a common devotional format in which the patron seeks heavenly intercession. Saint Anthony the Hermit (c. 251-356), the father of Christian monasticism, withdrew to the Egyptian desert to battle spiritual forces — his life exemplifying Paul's exhortation: 'Put on the full armor of God, so that you can take your stand against the devil's schemes' (Ephesians 6:11). Saint Cornelius, Pope and martyr (d. 253), led the Church during the Decian persecution and upheld the readmission of lapsed Christians — embodying the pastoral mercy of Christ the Good Shepherd. Mary Magdalene, holding her ointment jar, represents the penitent sinner who found forgiveness at Jesus's feet: 'Her many sins have been forgiven — as her great love has shown' (Luke 7:47). She became the first witness of the Resurrection (John 20:11-18). Together these three saints represent the monastic, episcopal, and penitent dimensions of the Christian life — withdrawal and spiritual warfare, leadership and mercy, repentance and devoted love. The kneeling donor places himself under their spiritual protection, embodying the communion of saints: the living and the dead united in Christ.

scripture_refs.reason: Spiritual armor against the devil — Anthony's legendary battles with demons in the desert
Great love from great forgiveness — Mary Magdalene's story embodied in her presence
Magdalene as first witness of the Resurrection — her honored status among the saints
The great cloud of witnesses surrounding the faithful — the donor seeking saintly intercession

_meta.title: Sts Anthony the Hermit, Cornelius and Mary Magdalen with a Donor
